title: rhynchosinapis cheiranthos subsp. montana
intro: Rhynchosinapis cheiranthos subsp. montana, also known as Mountain Rocket Cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a wild relative of the garden cress, used in various herbal preparations for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Rhynchosinapis cheiranthos subsp. montana can be consumed as a leafy green or used in herbal preparations like teas, salves, and infusions to harness its health benefits.
